channel.title: Remote-Work on Work From Home Pro
channel.description: Recent content in Remote-Work on Work From Home Pro

item.title: Best Home Office Desk Essentials for Your First Remote Job (2026)
item.description:
My first remote job started on a dining table. Laptop balanced on a stack of books, a kitchen chair that destroyed my back within two weeks, video calls where coworkers could see my refrigerator. I figured it out eventually — but I spent money in the wrong order and bought things I didn’t need before I bought things I did.

This guide is what I wish someone had handed me on day one.

item.title: Best Monitors for Working From Home Under $300 (2026)
item.description:
The laptop screen is the enemy of your neck. It’s too low, too small, and it forces you to look down for eight hours a day. The fix is simple and it doesn’t have to cost much.

I’ve been through four monitors in six years. One had terrible color. One died after 14 months. Two are still running. Here’s what I know now.

⚡ Quick Picks

#ProductWhy We Like ItPrice
1 LG 27MN60T-B Best Overall 27 inch Under $200 $189
2 Dell S2722DC Best USB-C Monitor Under $300 $279
3 LG 24MK430H-B Best Budget 24 inch $149
4 Samsung LS27R354FHNXZA Best for Video Calls Under $200 $179
5 AOC 24B2XHM2 Best Ultra-Budget Under $130 $129

Why Your Monitor Matters More Than You Think

Two things happen when your monitor is wrong:

item.title: How to Set Up a Home Office for Under $1,000 (That Doesn't Suck)
item.description:
My first home office was a kitchen table and a folding chair. My back hurt within a week. Six years later — and after spending way too much on gear I didn’t need and not enough on gear I did — here’s what I’d tell myself if I could do it over.

You don’t need $3,000 worth of equipment. You need the right $700-900 worth.

What’s Actually Causing Your Discomfort

Before buying anything: most home office discomfort comes from three things, in order of impact:
